title: papaver rhoeas subsp. strigosum
intro: Papaver rhoeas subsp. strigosum is a subspecies of the poppy plant, also known as the corn poppy or field poppy, a delicate annual plant with bright red flowers and papery seed pods. The medicinal properties of Papaver rhoeas subsp. strigosum include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for insomnia, anxiety, and menstrual cramps. Papaver rhoeas subsp. strigosum can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or oil, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
